comment: Hand-authored Act-1 dialogue (takes precedence over the generated Drifella barks in content/drifella-barks.json). Two passes: (1) the opening-house cast (npcId->character is a first pass to refine during sprite work, e.g. Biscuit on the dog sprite); (2) a Signal Town MILADY-LORE pass on reachable path NPCs (166/167/191/1410/163/136/202/138/146/1411/135/137) seeding the antagonist's lore through townsfolk rumor: Little Swag World is the organic source, Milady/Remilia corrupts it into hostile classification infrastructure (provenance gates, the public version, the Malady as visible symptom). See docs/swagbound-eb-act1-arc-mapping.md.

pages: Milady? Yeah, she's like, the brand. It's on everything down here. I stopped reading it.
Probably fine. It's usually fine. Why, did you read it?
My feed says Milady loves us. My feed also says I won a boat.
I did not win a boat. But I keep checking, you know? Just in case the boat's real and the love isn't.
Everyone north of here works for Milady. Nobody north of here will say so.
That's the benefit package. You stop being able to say it, and in exchange you stop having to.
I walked up the line once to ask Milady a question.
The line asked me three back, kept the answers, and mailed me a cleaner version of the question. Never went again.
The Malady's already up there past the gate, wearing a face.
Could be anyone's. Could be the better-lit version of yours. That's the part nobody likes to test.
Past the gate the air starts itemizing you. Little ticks, like a register that can see.
That's the threshold. That's where Milady stops being a rumor and becomes a form you have to sign.
Milady doesn't run Signal Town. The town runs ON her, like a charge nobody itemized.
You'll meet her paperwork long before you meet her. Most people stop at the paperwork.
I got FREAKING FIRED FROM MILADY'S for asking what the job actually was.
Turned out the job was being me, at scale, badly. They had a cleaner me by lunch.
Used to be Little Swag World was just where we were from. No stamp, no fee.
Then Milady filed it as a 'source' and started charging us rent on ourselves. She calls that provenance.
The gate isn't cruel. It just needs you to prove normal, show left, match background.
Milady wrote the form. Nobody has ever passed it clean. That part isn't a bug.
Remilia's the one holding the stamp.
Milady's the one who decided there'd BE a stamp. Different job. Same smirk.
Seen your public version yet? Smiles better than you. Answers before you do.
Milady prefers it, honestly. Less upkeep, no opinions of its own.
Don't stare at the cards too long. The cards read you back.
Whatever they find, they file straight up the line. Everything up the line is Milady's.
They say Milady was born down in the source somewhere. They say she's already pregnant with the next one.
They say a lot. The saying sticks better than the truth. That stickiness IS the machine.
That Malady up the route? That's only the part you're allowed to see.
Milady's the part already inside your distribution. You can't out-run your own reach.
My cousin tried to opt out of provenance.
Now there's a cleaner cousin downtown, doing my cousin better than he ever did. We don't speak. He files reports.
Everything here points up the line. Up the line is the yard, and the yard is Milady's.
And Milady's is everywhere, which is exactly how you know it's nowhere good.
I'm not scared of Milady.
I'm scared of how reasonable she makes the paperwork sound. You sign before you notice you agreed.
"Hotel Onett. One night re-syncs the whole party — HP, PP, anyone who fainted. Runs you $100."
...you crash hard. Somewhere a save chime plays.
"Morning, champ. Everybody's topped off. Door's that way."
MiFella lets himself in like he owns the version of you that counts.
"Bosch! Wake up. You're trending before breakfast. Congrats, I think?"
"There's already a you outside. Cleaner haircut, smoother story."
"He signed a 4am mint: 'Swag is eternal.' The town's already calling him convenient."
"Relax, it's free press. Come let it count. ...You're gonna make a face, aren't you. They love the face."
The intake printer blinks: ROUTE FATIGUE ACCEPTED.
"Clean breath, Bosch. I can reset your meters, not your story."
Guardian checks the door latch, then Bosch's pulse.
"Rest here. The house will hold your place while the route tries to edit it."
The Bosch Terminal boots from a timestamp Bosch has not reached yet.
"Bosch, the route is not neutral. A public version is already moving through town ahead of your body."
"That version is the Malady: symptom, mask, and civic convenience with your smile stapled on."
"Milady is the engine underneath it. Rumor, paperwork, feed pressure, all one cheap machine."
"Walk the route anyway. Make the public version prove provenance in daylight."
MiFella hits the door like the algorithm owes him rent.
"Bosch. Up. You're trending before breakfast, which is legally the worst kind of trending."
"Some public version of you is already outside, polite and wrong. Town's eating it up."
"Bring shoes. Bring a face you can prove is yours. The trouble is making good time."
The Public Version Clique doesn't crowd you. It arranges itself around you, smiling, like a search result that already knows your name.
"Relax, Bosch. We did the hard part. There's a cleaner you, signed and ready. Just nod and let this one count."
"Refuse and you stop being a person and start being a dispute. ...Oh, you're going to dispute it. Adorable. Cataloged."
They step into the route like they own the receipt. Whatever you do next, they'll file as the fight starting.
